input.instructions:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B2-usaco-2023-us-open-gold-tree-merging
Title: B2. USACO 2023 US Open Gold / Tree Merging

Problem:
Reconstruct a valid sequence of sibling-merge operations that transforms an initial rooted tree into a final rooted tree.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

variables.selected_prompt_text:
Write a draft answer and send one reviewer child a focused request to test the weakest assumptions, check small candidate cases, and correct the draft if needed.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "review",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "draft_response": "plain-text draft answer",
  "message_for_reviewer": "plain-text review request for the child"
}
variables.child_selected_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>\nJUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>",
  "result_type": "summary"
}

orchestration.decision_notes: The child result provides a conceptual solution to the problem. I will format it into the required final response structure.

Map each child of the target node to a contiguous range of children of the original node. For each target child, merge all original children in its assigned range into a single node using sibling-merge operations. Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-B2-usaco-2023-us-open-gold-tree-merging
Title: B2. USACO 2023 US Open Gold / Tree Merging

Problem:
Reconstruct a valid sequence of sibling-merge operations that transforms an initial rooted tree into a final rooted tree.

Grading target:
Binary hidden-test acceptance.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

results.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: To solve the problem, perform a post-order traversal of the target tree. For each node in the target tree, identify the corresponding node in the original tree. Map each child of the target node to a contiguous range of children of the original node. For each target child, merge all original children in its assigned range into a single node using sibling-merge operations. This is possible if and only if the target tree is a contraction of the original tree where each target node represents a subtree of the original tree and children are merged only among siblings.

JUSTIFICATION: Bottom-up merging ensures that subtrees are fully formed and consolidated before their parent nodes are processed, maintaining the structural integrity required by the sibling-merge operation rules.
